channel.title: Mokshify Engineering
channel.description: Architecture writing, decision records and build-in-public notes from Mokshify, an AI-first software studio in Hyderabad.

item.title: RAG, explained like we build it
item.description: Chunking decides quality, pgvector in the product database, and when to skip RAG entirely.

item.title: LLM evaluation: tests for the non-deterministic
item.description: Real-case test sets, fact-level assertions, and eval gates in CI.

item.title: AWS vs Azure vs OCI vs GCP - an honest matrix
item.description: What actually differs, what does not, and the five-step decision procedure.

item.title: Observability: knowing before the customer does
item.description: Logs first, metrics that gate, traces when earned, alerts that respect humans.

item.title: Monolith vs microservices - the honest decision
item.description: When a modular monolith wins, when services earn their operational cost, and the migration path between them.

item.title: Authentication architecture
item.description: Tokens, sessions, rotation and audit - the front door done properly, plus the honest MVP shortcut.

item.title: Multi-tenant SaaS architecture
item.description: Row-level vs schema vs database-per-tenant, with the promotion escape hatch.

item.title: Caching strategy, layer by layer
item.description: Browser to CDN to Redis to Postgres - and the rules that keep invalidation sane.

item.title: Background jobs and queues
item.description: Idempotency, retries, dead letters - and when Redis queues beat a real broker.

item.title: Journal 001: streaming 914 frames without a framework
item.description: A 97.7% payload reduction and the lessons of a scroll-scrubbed film.

item.title: Journal 002: five bugs worth writing down
item.description: Real defects from building this site, each generalised into a lesson.
